steps.title: Identify the decay factor
steps.content: The beam starts with $800$ photons. A decrease of $50\%$ means the amount is multiplied by $0.5$ for each full $7$ millimeters.

steps.title: Count the intervals
steps.content:
At a depth of $x$ millimeters, the number of $7$-millimeter intervals is
$$\frac{x}{7}$$
So the exponent must be $\frac{x}{7}$.

steps.title: Build the model
steps.content:
Use the initial amount, decay factor, and interval count.
$$I=800(0.5)^{\frac{x}{7}}$$
The answer is D.
